channel.title: Artificial intelligence

item.title: CoreWeave shares drop after bigger than expected losses
item.description: Data centre group has ridden wave of enthusiasm for artificial intelligence

item.title: Routine AI assistance hits skills of health experts performing colonoscopies
item.description: Study comes amid rapid adoption of the fast-developing technology

item.title: Altman challenges Musk with plans for Neuralink rival
item.description: OpenAI in talks to invest in brain implant start-up that would compete with Musk’s company

item.title: Anthropic offers Claude chatbot to US lawmakers for $1
item.description: Group goes beyond rival OpenAI in making enterprise tools available to all three government branches

item.title: What are the limits of the AI mathematician?
item.description: If models can learn to master complex calculations, they could solve problems that have so far eluded us

item.title: Beijing demands Chinese tech giants justify purchases of Nvidia’s H20 chips
item.description: Regulators ask companies including Alibaba and ByteDance why they do not use domestic alternatives

item.title: Musk threatens to sue Apple over App Store rankings
item.description: Billionaire accuses tech group of unfairly favouring rival OpenAI

item.title: The lost art of admitting what you don’t know
item.description: Even LLMs are starting to show this worrying human tendency

item.title: India’s blended fuel success angers consumers
item.description: Also in this newsletter: Atul Soneja’s mantra, and has the IT sector already lost the AI race?

item.title: Trump opens door to Nvidia selling advanced AI chips to China for a fee
item.description: US president confirms he negotiated ‘a little deal’ with chipmaker’s chief Jensen Huang

item.title: Manus and Benchmark: the AI deal that upset China and the US
item.description: Start-up came under fire from Beijing for abandoning the country as Washington scrutinised its Silicon Valley backer

item.title: South Korea’s Upstage enters global AI race
item.description: Start-up has produced a large language model that performs as well as advanced systems in the US and China

item.title: China wants US to relax export controls on chips as part of trade deal
item.description: Experts warn dropping restrictions would give Beijing a competitive advantage in artificial intelligence

item.title: Can we just have one day when no one mentions AI?
item.description: The gap between hype and reality is frustratingly wide and growing

item.title: India’s IT services groups race to reinvent themselves for AI age
item.description: Outsourcing sector struggles to adjust as clients shift budgets to artificial intelligence projects

item.title: Can OpenAI’s GPT-5 model live up to sky-high expectations?
item.description: Release of eagerly awaited system upgrade has been met with a mixed response, with some users calling the gains ‘modest’

item.title: The fund manager of the future might just be a machine
item.description: What if AI doesn’t just enhance active investing, but renders much of it obsolete?

item.title: AI is coming for (some) finance jobs
item.description: The coming quant crunch?

item.title: Could AI help America out of its debt hole?
item.description: Some investors believe the technology’s productivity benefits will put the US on a better fiscal path

item.title: HMRC must disclose whether it used AI in tax credit decisions, court rules
item.description: Revenue had rejected transparency request from tax adviser who believed it used technology to reject R&D applications

item.title: Axes of Evil: ChartCrimeGPT
item.description: Y oh Y

item.title: OpenAI unveils long-awaited GPT-5 model
item.description: Chief Sam Altman hailed next-generation system as ‘major upgrade’

item.title: The risk of putting the ‘open’ back into OpenAI
item.description: Following China’s success, US companies are hoping to defend the centrality of their commercial models

item.title: Online learning stocks deserve a better grade
item.description: Education apps should be a beneficiary of AI, retooling some workers and helping others to keep jobs they already have

item.title: How more efficient data centres could unlock the AI boom
item.description: Efficiency is becoming a bigger priority as tech companies confront strained energy resources
